MBBS in Kazakhstan

  • Home
  • Kazakhstan

Kazakhstan

Study MBBS in Kazakhstan for Indian Students

Kazakhstan is a popular choice for Indian students who want to study MBBS abroad. The country offers high-quality medical education at an affordable cost, with globally recognized degrees. The MBBS course in Kazakhstan lasts for 6 years, including an internship. Many medical universities in Kazakhstan are approved by NMC, WHO, USMLE, GMC, and IMED, allowing students to practice medicine in India and other countries.

Universities In Kazakhstan?

About Kazakhstan

Kazakhstan is the largest country in Central Asia and the 9th largest in the world. It shares borders with Russia, China, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, and Turkmenistan. The country has a rich history, diverse culture, and strong education system, making it a preferred destination for international students.

Kazakhstan’s medical universities are globally recognized and offer high-quality education at affordable costs. With modern infrastructure, experienced faculty, and English-taught MBBS programs, it attracts thousands of students every year. The country is also known for its safe environment, welcoming people, and growing economy, making it an ideal place for students to live and study.

High-Quality Education

Kazakhstan has well-established medical universities that follow international standards. Many universities are recognized by WHO, NMC, USMLE, and GMC, ensuring global acceptance of the MBBS degree.

Affordable Fees

Compared to private medical colleges in India and other countries, MBBS in Kazakhstan is much more affordable. The tuition fees range from $2,000 to $6,000 per year, making it a budget-friendly option for Indian students.

English-Medium Programs

Most medical universities in Kazakhstan offer MBBS courses in English, so Indian students can study without any language barrier. However, learning some basic Kazakh or Russian can be helpful for daily life and internships.

Eligibility

Eligibility to Study MBBS in Kazakhstan for Indian Students

Indian students who want to study MBBS in Kazakhstan must meet the following eligibility criteria:

NEET Qualification

Educational Qualification – Students must have completed 12th grade with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB). General category: Minimum 50% aggregate in PCB. Reserved category (SC/ST/OBC): Minimum 40% aggregate in PCB.

NEET Qualification

Indian students must qualify NEET to be eligible for admission and to practice medicine in India after completing MBBS. NEET scores are valid for 3 years for MBBS admissions abroad.

Age Requirement

The student must be at least 17 years old as of December 31 of the admission year.

No Entrance Exam

Unlike India, no additional entrance test is required for MBBS admission in Kazakhstan.

Valid Documents

Students must have a valid passport, academic certificates, and other necessary documents for admission.

Is Kazakhstan Safe for Indian Medical Students?

Yes, Kazakhstan is a safe country for Indian medical students. The country has a stable government, low crime rate, and a welcoming atmosphere for international students. Here’s why Kazakhstan is considered a safe destination:

  • Peaceful & Politically Stable – Kazakhstan is known for its friendly international relations and stable government, ensuring a secure environment for students.

  • Safe University Campuses – Medical universities in Kazakhstan provide secure hostel facilities, CCTV surveillance, and strict campus security to protect students.

  • Student-Friendly Environment – Kazakh people are friendly and welcoming towards Indian students, making it easy to adjust to the new culture.

  • Low Crime Rate – Compared to many other countries, Kazakhstan has a low crime rate, especially in student areas.

  • Indian Community & Support – Many Indian students study in Kazakhstan, and universities often have Indian student associations to provide support.

By following basic safety precautions and carrying necessary ID documents, students can have a comfortable and secure stay.

Advantages of Studying MBBS in Kazakhstan

Studying MBBS in Kazakhstan is a great option for Indian students because of its affordable fees, high-quality education, and global recognition. Here are some key advantages:

1. Affordable Tuition Fees

MBBS in Kazakhstan is much cheaper than private medical colleges in India, with fees ranging from $2,000 to $6,000 per year.

2. Globally Recognized Degree

Medical universities in Kazakhstan are approved by NMC, WHO, USMLE, GMC, and IMED, allowing students to practice medicine in India and other countries.

3. No Donation or Capitation Fee

Unlike private medical colleges in India, admission is purely based on merit, with no extra donation required.

4. English-Medium Instruction

Most medical universities offer MBBS courses in English, so Indian students don’t face a language barrier.

5. High-Quality Education & Modern Infrastructure

Universities have well-equipped labs, advanced hospitals, and experienced faculty for practical training.

6. Simple Admission Process –

Students can get admission easily with 12th-grade marks and NEET qualification, without any additional entrance exams.

7. Safe & Student-Friendly Environment

Kazakhstan is a safe country with a welcoming atmosphere and a large community of international students.

8. Opportunity for Part-Time Work

Some students can work part-time to gain practical experience and manage expenses.

9. Good Career Prospects

After completing MBBS in Kazakhstan, graduates can appear for FMGE (India), USMLE (USA), PLAB (UK), or other licensing exams to practice medicine globally.

10. Budget Friendly Accomodation

MBBS in Kazakhstan is much cheaper than private medical colleges in India.

LOAN

Education Loan for Indian Students to Study MBBS in Kazakhstan

Indian students who want to study MBBS in Kazakhstan can apply for an education loan to cover tuition fees, hostel charges, and other expenses. Many banks and financial institutions in India provide loans for studying abroad.

  • NEET Qualification – Students must qualify NEET to be eligible for an education loan.

  • University Recognition – The university should be approved by NMC and WHO for the loan to be considered.

  • Loan Amount – Depending on the university and course fees, students can get loans ranging from ₹10 to ₹30 lakhs.

  • Collateral Requirement :

  • Loans below ₹7.5 lakhs may not need collateral.

  • Loans below ₹7.5 lakhs may not need collateral.

  • Parent/Guardian’s Financial Background – Banks check the income stability and credit history of parents/guardians for loan approval.

  • Documents Required for Education Loan

  • Documents Required for Education Loan

🔹 Admission Letter from a recognized medical university in Kazakhstan.
🔹 NEET Scorecard to prove eligibility.
🔹 Fee Structure issued by the university.
🔹 KYC Documents (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport).
🔹 Income Proof (Salary slips, ITR, Bank statements).
🔹 Collateral Documents (if applicable).

Loan Repayment Terms

Repayment starts after course completion, usually with a moratorium period of 6-12 months.

The loan can be repaid in EMIs over 5-15 years, depending on the bank.

💡 Note: At Atlas Mentor, our counselors assist students in securing education loans for MBBS abroad, making the process smooth and hassle-free.

F. A. Q

Frequently Asked Questions on MBBS in Kazakhstan

If you are planning to study MBBS in Kazakhstan, you might have several questions in mind. Here are the most commonly asked questions and their answers to help you make an informed decision.

What is the duration of MBBS in Kazakhstan?

The MBBS course in Kazakhstan is 6 years, including 5 years of classroom learning and 1 year of internship.

Is an MBBS degree from Kazakhstan valid in India?

Yes, MBBS degrees from NMC-approved universities in Kazakhstan are valid in India. However, graduates must pass the FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduate Examination) to practice in India.

What is the cost of studying MBBS in Kazakhstan?

The tuition fees range from $2,000 to $6,000 per year, depending on the university. Hostel and living expenses are also affordable compared to private medical colleges in India.

Is NEET required for admission to MBBS in Kazakhstan?

Yes, Indian students must qualify the NEET exam to get admission and to be eligible to practice medicine in India after graduation.

Can students work part-time while studying MBBS in Kazakhstan?

Yes, students can work part-time while studying, but their main focus should be on their medical education.
